layman_headline: The market is scared and most coins look beat up — patience is the play right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is sitting in 'Fear' territory at 28/100, meaning most investors are nervous. Bitcoin is holding steady around $64,500 but it's hogging all the attention — altcoins are struggling. Most of the coins on our radar are in downtrends with bearish chart patterns. While a few like ETH and PEPE are approaching oversold levels, none have shown a convincing reversal signal yet. Jumping in now would be catching a falling knife.
layman_action: Wait on the sidelines. Set price alerts for ETH near $1,800 support — if it bounces there with volume, that could be our entry. Don't chase anything in a fearful, alt-unfriendly market.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate presents significant headwinds: BONK is in freefall with a -2.0 chart bias and Three Black Crows pattern — textbook avoid. KAITO is overbought at RSI 79 after a 45% weekly run with terrible risk/reward (0.46x). LDO has the best chart bias (+0.85) with Three White Soldiers, but RSI at 59 after a 15% weekly move and 0.38x risk/reward makes chasing unattractive. ETH is the most compelling setup with RSI at 39 (our highest-confidence signal type at 80% win rate), but the chart bias is -0.70 with price below all major MAs. BTC dominance at 56.5% actively suppresses alt performance. The prudent move is to wait for ETH to test and confirm $1,800 support with a reversal candle before committing capital.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to drain capital from altcoins — any further BTC strength could push alts lower
Fear & Greed at 28 could deteriorate further if macro news turns negative, dragging all risk assets down
ETH below all major moving averages with -0.70 chart bias — the downtrend could accelerate rather than reverse
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce with volume spike would trigger a BUY signal
Fear & Greed index movement — a shift above 35 would signal improving sentiment for alt entries
BTC dominance — any decline below 55.5% would unlock better alt trading conditions
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear-driven consolidation phase with BTC holding near $64,500 while altcoins broadly underperform. High BTC dominance at 56.5% creates a hostile environment for alt swing trades, and the GENIUS Act stablecoin deadline miss adds regulatory uncertainty.
context_banner: Fear grips the alt market while Bitcoin dominance squeezes alternatives — disciplined traders wait for confirmed support bounces before deploying capital.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold — historically our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but hasn't crossed the 30 threshold yet for a high-conviction entry.
Market Fear at 28/100 trending flat from 25 — indicates depressed sentiment that often precedes bounces, but hasn't shown a turning point yet.
US agencies missing the GENIUS Act stablecoin deadline adds regulatory uncertainty, potentially keeping institutional capital sidelined and suppressing risk appetite.
All candidates except LDO show bearish or strongly bearish chart bias scores — the broad technical picture argues against aggressive positioning.
ETH derivatives reset narrative suggests positioning is being cleaned up, which could set the stage for a healthier move once support is confirmed.
